title: Sizzling French Cabbage Skillet
description: A hearty, monsoon-inspired French cabbage skillet with a deep burgundy hue, seasoned with wine, tomato paste, and aromatic herbs, perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat and add the vegetable oil and duck fat until shimmering.
Add the sliced onion and sauté for 3 minutes until translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the shredded cabbage, a pinch of salt, and pepper, then sauté for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the cabbage starts to wilt.
Stir in the tomato paste and smoked paprika, coating the vegetables evenly for 1 minute.
Pour in the white wine, bring to a gentle simmer, and let it reduce by half, about 4 minutes.
Add the beef broth and dried thyme, cover the skillet with a lid, and simmer for 10 minutes until the cabbage is tender and flavors meld.
Remove the lid, stir in fresh parsley if using, and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Serve hot straight from the skillet, optionally with crusty bread.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
